game_info.title: Poly Bridge 3D
game_info.concept:
**Poly Bridge 2:** Poly Bridge 2 is a physics-based puzzle game where players construct bridges to guide vehicles across gaps, using a drag-and-drop interface to place structural elements like roads, wood, steel, and the new Spring material between fixed anchor points. Players select materials from a palette and draw segments by tapping and dragging between nodes, forming a network of supports and roadways, with each material having specific cost and stress resistance properties. After construction, players initiate a simulation phase by tapping a "Start" button, observing how vehicles (e.g., cars, trucks) attempt to cross the bridge, with structural integrity determined by a custom physics engine that calculates stress on each segment. A level is successfully completed when all designated vehicles safely reach their destination without the bridge collapsing, adhering to a strict budget limit (e.g., $10,000) and a maximum number of structural elements. Progression involves completing over 100 pre-designed levels, each introducing new environmental challenges, vehicle types, and mechanics like hydraulic pistons or the bouncy Spring material, with difficulty scaling through increasingly complex terrain and tighter budget constraints.

App Store URL: https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/poly-bridge-2/id1524371466
App Store ID: 1524371466

================================================================================
Full Description:
================================================================================

The acclaimed bridge-building simulator is back and better than ever!

New levels, new mechanics, a custom physics engine, and much more! Rediscover your engineering creativity with even more hours of puzzling fun!

Adrian Talens returns with a new full-length Soundtrack! Relax to the soothing and critically acclaimed music with 13 completely new tracks plus 18 of the original Poly Bridge songs remastered! Enjoy over an hour and a half of the familiar and gentle acoustic guitar rhythms.

Take on levels with some added cushion and bounce your way to victory with the new Spring material. Now your bridges can be even more flexible!

We went above and beyond this time, crafting a custom physics engine optimized and fine-tuned specifically for bridge simulations. Accurate and predictable, it guarantees the same simulation outcome for everyone, keeping the competitive aspect of the game alive!
game_info.description: Poly Bridge 3D is a physics-based engineering puzzle game where you construct bridges to help a vehicle cross a gap. Use the keyboard to design your bridge structure using various materials like Roads, Wood, Steel, and Springs. Manage your budget and stress loads to ensure the bridge holds up under the weight of the crossing vehicle!
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Keys: Move Construction Cursor
- Shift + Arrow Keys: Fast Cursor Movement
- Space: Place Node / Start Segment / Finish Segment
- Z: Delete Node or Segment under cursor
- 1: Select Road Material (Driveable)
- 2: Select Wood Material (Support)
- 3: Select Steel Material (Strong Support)
- 4: Select Spring Material (Flexible)
- Enter: Start Simulation / Play
- R: Reset Simulation (Back to Build Mode) / Restart Level
- ESC: Pause / Unpause

TEST_1.test_description: Verify basic bridge construction and physics stability.
TEST_1.strategy_description: In the first level, programmatically construct a simple horizontal road bridge connecting the two anchor points using the 'Road' material. Start the simulation.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The bridge should support its own weight without collapsing immediately upon simulation start. The vehicle should land on it and begin moving.

TEST_2.test_description: Verify vehicle interaction and win condition.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Build a fully supported bridge (road deck + wood truss underneath) on Level 1. Start simulation and wait for the car to cross.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The vehicle successfully traverses the gap and reaches the flag, triggering the 'GAME_OVER_WIN' state.
TEST_2.description: Verify vehicle interaction and win condition.

TEST_3.strategy_description: Build a single long, unsupported road segment (or a chain of them) that is structurally unsound. Start simulation.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The bridge segments should turn red (indicating high stress) and break (disappear or detach) causing the vehicle to fall, triggering 'GAME_OVER_LOSE'.
TEST_3.strategy: Build a single long, unsupported road segment (or a chain of them) that is structurally unsound. Start simulation.
TEST_3.expected: The bridge segments should turn red (indicating high stress) and break (disappear or detach) causing the vehicle to fall, triggering 'GAME_OVER_LOSE'.

TEST_4.test_description: Verify material switching and budget constraints.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Attempt to build segments using different materials (Road, Wood, Steel). Check if the budget counter decreases appropriately for each.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: The 'currentBudget' variable in gameState should decrease by the correct cost amount for each placed segment. Construction should be blocked if budget is exceeded.
TEST_4.description: Verify material switching and budget constraints.

TEST_5.test_description: Verify collision detection between vehicle and road.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Build a ramp (sloped road). Start simulation.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: The vehicle should rotate and climb the slope, demonstrating that wheel collision with line segments is functioning correctly.
TEST_5.description: Verify collision detection between vehicle and road.
